fields.Evidence / Notes: Source: Scribe observation 1 from 2026-05-07. After Elastic alert upload, Gemini Agent emitted fenced YAML blocks containing internal readiness, normalized evidence, routing, scope recommendations, query-planning notes, and output-composer instructions before the final SOC-facing answer. Prior defect manifested as JSON leakage; current iteration leaks YAML instead. Root issue is internal prime/sub-agent handoff/state being exposed to the operator. Expected behavior: operator sees only final analyst-facing output needed for triage: BLUF, facts, analysis, validated query, and analyst scratchpad. Recommended next-iteration fix: deterministic final output gate/sanitizer; final composer owns all operator-visible text; block fenced internal yaml/json and internal keys such as readiness_confirmed, immediate_work_family, normalized_evidence, notes_for_query_planner, notes_for_output_composer, selected_command_family, candidate_datasets_ranked. Regression test: upload Elastic alert and assert final response contains no internal YAML/JSON/state blocks. Severity: critical.
fields.Repo Path or Skill: Gemini Agent framework / output composer / response sanitizer
fields.Work Item: Fix Gemini Agent internal handoff/state leakage in operator output

fields.Evidence / Notes: Created from Idea Inbox record recKgTM6g9nLVIRsQ / idea_id=IDEA-20260422-GH-SHA-MISMATCH-RECOVERY. Idea detail: During live Gemini governed-source repair, a GitHub `update_file` attempt failed with a 409 SHA mismatch because the file had advanced beyond the earlier fetched snapshot. The correct recovery was not to treat this as a mysterious blocker. The right bounded recovery is: refetch the exact live file from `main`, take the new blob SHA as authoritative, rebase the prepared edit onto that current content, and retry the write against the refreshed SHA. Treat this as a normal connector write-race or st
